title: chrysopogon aciculatus var. echinulatus
intro: Chrysopogon aciculatus var. echinulatus, also known as East Indian lemongrass, is a variant of the lemongrass plant that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which is rich in citral and has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of East Indian lemongrass include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. East Indian lemongrass can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or added to skincare products, making it a versatile and valuable herb for promoting overall well-being.
